description: tarot cards and their meanings upright and reversed, plus a short description

cards.name: The Fool
cards.upright: New beginnings, innocence, spontaneity, adventure, taking a leap of faith.
cards.reversed: Recklessness, naivety, poor planning, hesitation, foolish risks.
cards.description: The Fool symbolizes the start of a journey and embracing the unknown with trust and optimism.

cards.name: The Magician
cards.upright: Manifestation, resourcefulness, inspired action, skill, power.
cards.reversed: Manipulation, poor planning, untapped talents, illusion.
cards.description: The Magician represents turning ideas into reality through focus, confidence, and action.

cards.name: The High Priestess
cards.upright: Intuition, mystery, inner wisdom, spiritual insight, subconscious knowledge.
cards.reversed: Hidden motives, blocked intuition, confusion, secrets revealed.
cards.description: The High Priestess encourages trusting your intuition and looking beneath the surface.

cards.name: The Empress
cards.upright: Abundance, nurturing, fertility, creativity, beauty.
cards.reversed: Creative block, dependence, smothering, lack of self-care.
cards.description: The Empress embodies growth, compassion, and the nurturing of ideas and relationships.

cards.name: The Emperor
cards.upright: Authority, structure, leadership, stability, discipline.
cards.reversed: Control issues, rigidity, domination, stubbornness.
cards.description: The Emperor represents order, responsibility, and strong foundations.

cards.name: The Hierophant
cards.upright: Tradition, spiritual wisdom, guidance, learning, conformity.
cards.reversed: Rebellion, unconventional thinking, personal beliefs, challenging traditions.
cards.description: The Hierophant reflects established systems, teachings, and shared beliefs.

cards.name: The Lovers
cards.upright: Love, harmony, partnership, alignment, meaningful choices.
cards.reversed: Disharmony, imbalance, conflict, separation, difficult decisions.
cards.description: The Lovers signify deep connections and choices aligned with personal values.

cards.name: The Chariot
cards.upright: Determination, willpower, success, ambition, self-control.
cards.reversed: Lack of direction, aggression, self-doubt, setbacks, loss of control.
cards.description: The Chariot symbolizes moving forward through focus, discipline, and perseverance.

cards.name: Strength
cards.upright: Courage, resilience, patience, compassion, inner strength.
cards.reversed: Self-doubt, insecurity, weakness, lack of confidence, fear.
cards.description: Strength reflects mastering challenges through calm confidence rather than force.

cards.name: The Hermit
cards.upright: Introspection, solitude, wisdom, self-discovery, reflection.
cards.reversed: Isolation, loneliness, withdrawal, avoidance, disconnection.
cards.description: The Hermit encourages looking inward for guidance, truth, and personal understanding.

cards.name: Wheel of Fortune
cards.upright: Luck, destiny, cycles, change, opportunity.
cards.reversed: Bad luck, resistance to change, delays, setbacks.
cards.description: The Wheel of Fortune reminds us that life is constantly changing and evolving.

cards.name: Justice
cards.upright: Fairness, truth, accountability, balance, integrity.
cards.reversed: Dishonesty, unfairness, avoidance of responsibility, imbalance.
cards.description: Justice emphasizes consequences, honesty, and objective decision-making.

cards.name: The Hanged Man
cards.upright: Perspective, surrender, patience, letting go, insight.
cards.reversed: Resistance, delay, indecision, stagnation.
cards.description: The Hanged Man invites a new perspective and acceptance of what cannot be controlled.

cards.name: Death
cards.upright: Transformation, endings, renewal, transition, release.
cards.reversed: Fear of change, stagnation, resistance, clinging to the past.
cards.description: Death symbolizes necessary endings that make way for growth and new beginnings.

cards.name: Temperance
cards.upright: Balance, moderation, patience, harmony, purpose.
cards.reversed: Excess, imbalance, impatience, extremes.
cards.description: Temperance encourages blending different aspects of life into harmony.

cards.name: The Devil
cards.upright: Attachment, temptation, materialism, unhealthy patterns, limitation.
cards.reversed: Breaking free, liberation, reclaiming power, overcoming addiction.
cards.description: The Devil highlights self-imposed restrictions and unhealthy attachments.

cards.name: The Tower
cards.upright: Sudden change, upheaval, revelation, awakening, disruption.
cards.reversed: Avoiding disaster, delayed change, fear of transformation.
cards.description: The Tower represents the collapse of false foundations and necessary breakthroughs.

cards.name: The Star
cards.upright: Hope, inspiration, healing, faith, renewal.
cards.reversed: Discouragement, doubt, lack of faith, pessimism.
cards.description: The Star brings optimism and reassurance after difficult times.

cards.name: The Moon
cards.upright: Intuition, illusion, dreams, uncertainty, mystery.
cards.reversed: Clarity, truth revealed, overcoming fear, understanding.
cards.description: The Moon explores the hidden aspects of the subconscious and emotions.

cards.name: The Sun
cards.upright: Joy, success, positivity, vitality, confidence.
cards.reversed: Temporary setbacks, unrealistic expectations, delayed happiness.
cards.description: The Sun radiates warmth, clarity, and positive outcomes.

cards.name: Judgement
cards.upright: Awakening, reflection, redemption, purpose, self-evaluation.
cards.reversed: Self-doubt, denial, avoidance, unwillingness to change.
cards.description: Judgement encourages honest reflection and embracing personal growth.

cards.name: The World
cards.upright: Completion, accomplishment, fulfillment, success, wholeness.
cards.reversed: Incomplete goals, delays, lack of closure, stagnation.
cards.description: The World signifies the successful completion of a cycle and readiness for the next.

cards.name: Ace of Cups
cards.upright: New love, emotional beginnings, compassion, creativity, openness.
cards.reversed: Blocked emotions, emptiness, emotional overwhelm, self-love needed.
cards.description: The Ace of Cups represents fresh emotional energy and the opening of the heart.

cards.name: Two of Cups
cards.upright: Partnership, mutual attraction, harmony, connection, agreement.
cards.reversed: Imbalance, separation, miscommunication, unequal feelings.
cards.description: The Two of Cups symbolizes emotional union, trust, and reciprocal connection.

cards.name: Three of Cups
cards.upright: Friendship, celebration, community, support, joy.
cards.reversed: Overindulgence, gossip, isolation, social tension.
cards.description: The Three of Cups highlights shared happiness, supportive bonds, and celebration.

cards.name: Four of Cups
cards.upright: Apathy, contemplation, reevaluation, emotional withdrawal.
cards.reversed: New awareness, renewed interest, acceptance, moving forward.
cards.description: The Four of Cups suggests pausing to reassess what truly fulfills you.

cards.name: Five of Cups
cards.upright: Loss, regret, grief, disappointment, focusing on what is gone.
cards.reversed: Acceptance, forgiveness, healing, seeing what remains.
cards.description: The Five of Cups reflects emotional pain while reminding you that not all is lost.

cards.name: Six of Cups
cards.upright: Nostalgia, childhood memories, innocence, kindness, reunion.
cards.reversed: Living in the past, unrealistic memories, emotional immaturity.
cards.description: The Six of Cups points to tenderness, memory, and the comfort of familiar connections.

cards.name: Seven of Cups
cards.upright: Choices, fantasy, imagination, possibilities, wishful thinking.
cards.reversed: Clarity, focus, realistic decisions, avoiding illusion.
cards.description: The Seven of Cups represents many options and the need to separate dreams from reality.

cards.name: Eight of Cups
cards.upright: Walking away, emotional growth, searching for deeper meaning.
cards.reversed: Fear of leaving, avoidance, stagnation, returning to old patterns.
cards.description: The Eight of Cups shows the courage to leave behind what no longer fulfills you.

cards.name: Nine of Cups
cards.upright: Contentment, satisfaction, wishes fulfilled, emotional pleasure.
cards.reversed: Dissatisfaction, greed, shallow happiness, unmet desires.
cards.description: The Nine of Cups represents personal fulfillment and enjoying emotional rewards.

cards.name: Ten of Cups
cards.upright: Happiness, family harmony, emotional fulfillment, lasting joy.
cards.reversed: Family conflict, broken harmony, unrealistic expectations.
cards.description: The Ten of Cups symbolizes deep emotional security and shared happiness.

cards.name: Page of Cups
cards.upright: Creative messages, curiosity, sensitivity, emotional openness.
cards.reversed: Emotional immaturity, insecurity, creative block, unrealistic feelings.
cards.description: The Page of Cups brings gentle emotional expression and imaginative possibilities.

cards.name: Knight of Cups
cards.upright: Romance, charm, idealism, following the heart, emotional pursuit.
cards.reversed: Moodiness, jealousy, unrealistic romance, empty promises.
cards.description: The Knight of Cups represents emotional movement, romantic gestures, and creative pursuit.

cards.name: Queen of Cups
cards.upright: Compassion, intuition, emotional maturity, nurturing, empathy.
cards.reversed: Emotional overwhelm, codependency, insecurity, poor boundaries.
cards.description: The Queen of Cups embodies deep emotional wisdom and compassionate care.

cards.name: King of Cups
cards.upright: Emotional balance, wisdom, diplomacy, compassion, calm leadership.
cards.reversed: Emotional manipulation, repression, moodiness, instability.
cards.description: The King of Cups represents emotional control, maturity, and steady compassion.

cards.name: Ace of Swords
cards.upright: Clarity, truth, breakthrough, new ideas, mental focus.
cards.reversed: Confusion, miscommunication, mental blocks, distorted truth.
cards.description: The Ace of Swords signals a powerful moment of insight and clear thinking.

cards.name: Two of Swords
cards.upright: Indecision, difficult choices, avoidance, emotional stalemate.
cards.reversed: Confusion lifting, decision made, facing the truth.
cards.description: The Two of Swords shows a blocked decision that requires honesty and clarity.

cards.name: Three of Swords
cards.upright: Heartbreak, sorrow, betrayal, grief, painful truth.
cards.reversed: Healing, forgiveness, recovery, releasing pain.
cards.description: The Three of Swords represents emotional pain and the process of confronting it.

cards.name: Four of Swords
cards.upright: Rest, recovery, contemplation, mental pause, healing.
cards.reversed: Burnout, restlessness, exhaustion, returning too soon.
cards.description: The Four of Swords advises stepping back to recover your energy and perspective.

cards.name: Five of Swords
cards.upright: Conflict, tension, winning at a cost, betrayal, ego battles.
cards.reversed: Reconciliation, making amends, moving past conflict.
cards.description: The Five of Swords highlights the cost of conflict and the need to choose battles wisely.

cards.name: Six of Swords
cards.upright: Transition, moving on, healing journey, leaving difficulty behind.
cards.reversed: Resistance to change, emotional baggage, unfinished business.
cards.description: The Six of Swords represents moving toward calmer waters after hardship.

cards.name: Seven of Swords
cards.upright: Strategy, secrecy, deception, independence, careful planning.
cards.reversed: Coming clean, exposure, self-deception, changing tactics.
cards.description: The Seven of Swords points to hidden motives, strategy, or the need for caution.

cards.name: Eight of Swords
cards.upright: Restriction, self-doubt, feeling trapped, limiting beliefs.
cards.reversed: Freedom, new perspective, release, empowerment.
cards.description: The Eight of Swords shows that perceived limitations may be stronger than real ones.

cards.name: Nine of Swords
cards.upright: Anxiety, worry, nightmares, guilt, mental distress.
cards.reversed: Hope, release, recovery, seeking support.
cards.description: The Nine of Swords reflects inner fear and the burden of overthinking.

cards.name: Ten of Swords
cards.upright: Painful ending, betrayal, rock bottom, finality, defeat.
cards.reversed: Recovery, survival, regeneration, lessons learned.
cards.description: The Ten of Swords marks a difficult ending that clears space for renewal.

cards.name: Page of Swords
cards.upright: Curiosity, ideas, communication, vigilance, learning.
cards.reversed: Gossip, defensiveness, scattered thoughts, hasty words.
cards.description: The Page of Swords represents mental curiosity and the urge to seek truth.

cards.name: Knight of Swords
cards.upright: Ambition, fast action, determination, direct communication.
cards.reversed: Impulsiveness, aggression, recklessness, rushing in.
cards.description: The Knight of Swords charges forward with intensity and mental drive.

cards.name: Queen of Swords
cards.upright: Independence, honesty, clear boundaries, intelligence, perception.
cards.reversed: Coldness, bitterness, harsh judgment, emotional distance.
cards.description: The Queen of Swords represents clear thinking, truth, and strong personal boundaries.

cards.name: King of Swords
cards.upright: Authority, logic, truth, discipline, intellectual power.
cards.reversed: Abuse of power, manipulation, cruelty, rigid thinking.
cards.description: The King of Swords symbolizes rational leadership and principled decision-making.

cards.name: Ace of Wands
cards.upright: Inspiration, new passion, creative spark, potential, energy.
cards.reversed: Delays, lack of motivation, creative block, missed opportunity.
cards.description: The Ace of Wands represents the first spark of passion, creativity, or action.

cards.name: Two of Wands
cards.upright: Planning, future vision, personal power, discovery, decisions.
cards.reversed: Fear of change, lack of planning, playing small, delays.
cards.description: The Two of Wands shows a moment of looking ahead and choosing your next direction.

cards.name: Three of Wands
cards.upright: Expansion, progress, foresight, opportunity, growth.
cards.reversed: Delays, limited vision, obstacles, frustration.
cards.description: The Three of Wands represents progress and waiting for your efforts to unfold.

cards.name: Four of Wands
cards.upright: Celebration, stability, home, community, joyful milestones.
cards.reversed: Instability, conflict at home, delayed celebration, tension.
cards.description: The Four of Wands symbolizes harmony, belonging, and shared achievement.

cards.name: Five of Wands
cards.upright: Competition, conflict, tension, challenge, differing opinions.
cards.reversed: Avoiding conflict, resolution, inner tension, compromise.
cards.description: The Five of Wands reflects friction that can push growth if handled constructively.

cards.name: Six of Wands
cards.upright: Victory, recognition, confidence, success, public praise.
cards.reversed: Ego, lack of recognition, self-doubt, private achievement.
cards.description: The Six of Wands represents success, visibility, and hard-earned recognition.

cards.name: Seven of Wands
cards.upright: Defensiveness, perseverance, standing your ground, courage.
cards.reversed: Giving up, overwhelm, vulnerability, feeling attacked.
cards.description: The Seven of Wands shows the need to protect your position and defend your progress.

cards.name: Eight of Wands
cards.upright: Speed, momentum, communication, travel, rapid progress.
cards.reversed: Delays, frustration, miscommunication, scattered energy.
cards.description: The Eight of Wands signals fast movement and events unfolding quickly.

cards.name: Nine of Wands
cards.upright: Resilience, persistence, boundaries, endurance, caution.
cards.reversed: Exhaustion, defensiveness, paranoia, giving up.
cards.description: The Nine of Wands represents strength after struggle and the will to keep going.

cards.name: Ten of Wands
cards.upright: Burden, responsibility, hard work, stress, carrying too much.
cards.reversed: Release, delegation, burnout, lightening the load.
cards.description: The Ten of Wands shows the weight of responsibility and the need to manage your energy.

cards.name: Page of Wands
cards.upright: Exploration, enthusiasm, creative ideas, curiosity, adventure.
cards.reversed: Lack of direction, impatience, immaturity, scattered inspiration.
cards.description: The Page of Wands brings youthful passion, discovery, and creative excitement.

cards.name: Knight of Wands
cards.upright: Action, passion, confidence, adventure, bold movement.
cards.reversed: Recklessness, impulsiveness, frustration, inconsistency.
cards.description: The Knight of Wands represents fiery momentum and the courage to act.

cards.name: Queen of Wands
cards.upright: Confidence, charisma, independence, warmth, determination.
cards.reversed: Insecurity, jealousy, demanding energy, self-doubt.
cards.description: The Queen of Wands embodies magnetic confidence and creative self-expression.

cards.name: King of Wands
cards.upright: Leadership, vision, confidence, entrepreneurship, boldness.
cards.reversed: Impulsiveness, arrogance, controlling behavior, unrealistic expectations.
cards.description: The King of Wands represents visionary leadership and decisive action.

cards.name: Ace of Pentacles
cards.upright: New opportunity, prosperity, stability, manifestation, material potential.
cards.reversed: Missed opportunity, financial delay, instability, poor planning.
cards.description: The Ace of Pentacles represents the seed of abundance, security, and practical growth.

cards.name: Two of Pentacles
cards.upright: Balance, adaptability, priorities, time management, flexibility.
cards.reversed: Overwhelm, imbalance, disorganization, poor priorities.
cards.description: The Two of Pentacles reflects juggling responsibilities and maintaining balance.

cards.name: Three of Pentacles
cards.upright: Teamwork, skill, collaboration, learning, craftsmanship.
cards.reversed: Poor teamwork, lack of effort, misalignment, low-quality work.
cards.description: The Three of Pentacles highlights collaboration, shared effort, and developing expertise.

cards.name: Four of Pentacles
cards.upright: Security, saving, control, stability, protecting resources.
cards.reversed: Greed, scarcity mindset, overspending, letting go.
cards.description: The Four of Pentacles shows attachment to security and the need to balance control with openness.

cards.name: Five of Pentacles
cards.upright: Hardship, financial struggle, insecurity, isolation, lack.
cards.reversed: Recovery, support, improvement, finding help.
cards.description: The Five of Pentacles reflects difficulty while reminding you that support may be available.

cards.name: Six of Pentacles
cards.upright: Generosity, giving, receiving, fairness, support.
cards.reversed: Unequal exchange, strings attached, debt, imbalance.
cards.description: The Six of Pentacles represents generosity, reciprocity, and balanced support.

cards.name: Seven of Pentacles
cards.upright: Patience, investment, long-term growth, evaluation, persistence.
cards.reversed: Impatience, poor results, wasted effort, lack of reward.
cards.description: The Seven of Pentacles encourages patience while assessing what your efforts are producing.

cards.name: Eight of Pentacles
cards.upright: Skill-building, dedication, craftsmanship, hard work, mastery.
cards.reversed: Perfectionism, lack of focus, repetitive work, low effort.
cards.description: The Eight of Pentacles represents committed practice and steady improvement.

cards.name: Nine of Pentacles
cards.upright: Independence, luxury, self-sufficiency, discipline, success.
cards.reversed: Overdependence, overspending, insecurity, false success.
cards.description: The Nine of Pentacles symbolizes earned comfort, confidence, and personal achievement.

cards.name: Ten of Pentacles
cards.upright: Legacy, wealth, family, long-term security, tradition.
cards.reversed: Family disputes, instability, financial loss, short-term thinking.
cards.description: The Ten of Pentacles represents lasting stability, inheritance, and shared prosperity.

cards.name: Page of Pentacles
cards.upright: Learning, ambition, study, practical opportunity, manifestation.
cards.reversed: Lack of progress, procrastination, poor planning, missed chances.
cards.description: The Page of Pentacles brings grounded curiosity and the desire to build something real.

cards.name: Knight of Pentacles
cards.upright: Hard work, routine, reliability, patience, responsibility.
cards.reversed: Stagnation, stubbornness, laziness, perfectionism.
cards.description: The Knight of Pentacles represents steady progress, discipline, and commitment.

cards.name: Queen of Pentacles
cards.upright: Nurturing, practicality, comfort, abundance, resourcefulness.
cards.reversed: Work-life imbalance, insecurity, neglect, overgiving.
cards.description: The Queen of Pentacles embodies grounded care, stability, and practical support.

cards.name: King of Pentacles
cards.upright: Wealth, stability, leadership, discipline, material success.
cards.reversed: Greed, stubbornness, corruption, obsession with status.
cards.description: The King of Pentacles represents mastery over material resources and long-term security.
